description_long: This stereo sound system features a rectangular body with a wood-textured casing and a metallic front panel. The front side is equipped with an array of knobs, buttons, and switches, as well as a central display area with indicator lights and dials, typical of vintage audio receivers. The top surface has a black mesh or grille, likely for ventilation. The rear panel is outfitted with various ports and connectors, including speaker terminals and input/output jacks, along with labeling for each connection. The sides are plain wood, matching the top and providing a classic, retro aesthetic. The overall build suggests a robust, high-quality device designed for home or studio audio use.
description: A vintage-style stereo sound system with a wood casing, metallic front panel full of controls, and multiple rear ports for audio connections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metallic control panel with numerous knobs, buttons, and a central display area.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wood-textured panel and the black mesh on top.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing various audio ports, connectors, and labeled input/output sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with a plain wood-textured panel and visible top mesh.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the front face, featuring a horizontal row of knobs and buttons, with a central display area flanked by indicator lights and labeled controls.
